articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Persistent Memory and Extended Reasoning Chains
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5 in late March 2026, featuring a dramatically improved architecture with native long-term memory across sessions and multi-step reasoning that outperforms GPT-4o on every major benchmark. The model introduces a new "deliberative alignment" mechanism that allows it to pause mid-generation and self-correct, significantly reducing hallucination rates in technical and scientific domains.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini 2.5 Pro Tops Agentic Coding Benchmarks
articles.summary: Google DeepMind's Gemini 2.5 Pro, released in early April 2026, set new state-of-the-art results on SWE-Bench and HumanEval-X, demonstrating the ability to autonomously resolve complex multi-file GitHub issues with over 60% accuracy. The model's 2-million-token context window and improved tool-use capabilities make it particularly effective for repository-scale code understanding and modification tasks.

articles.title: Figure Robotics Begins Commercial Deployment of Figure 02 Humanoids in BMW Factories
articles.summary: Figure AI announced the first large-scale commercial deployment of its Figure 02 humanoid robots at BMW's Spartanburg, South Carolina plant, with over 50 units now performing assembly-line logistics and parts inspection tasks. The robots leverage onboard vision-language models for flexible task adaptation, reportedly reducing the need for manual reprogramming when production lines change configurations.

articles.title: Tesla Optimus Gen 3 Demonstrates Autonomous Warehouse Navigation in Live Demo
articles.summary: Tesla showcased its third-generation Optimus humanoid performing end-to-end warehouse picking, packing, and sorting tasks without human teleoperation at a live event in Austin. The updated hardware features new actuators with 40% greater torque density and a custom Tesla-designed vision transformer chip enabling real-time 3D scene understanding at sub-100ms latency.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude's Tool-Use Orchestration Layer for Enterprise Agents
articles.summary: Anthropic released a new enterprise agent framework built on top of Claude that enables multi-step, multi-tool workflows with built-in audit logging, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, and granular permission scoping. The system is designed for regulated industries like finance and healthcare, where autonomous AI actions require verifiable chain-of-custody documentation for every decision made by the model.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 1.4nm Gate-All-Around Process Node
articles.summary: TSMC announced the start of volume production on its A14 (1.4nm-class) process using gate-all-around nanosheet transistors at its Fab 20 in Hsinchu, with initial capacity allocated to Apple and NVIDIA. The node delivers an estimated 20% speed improvement and 30% power reduction over N2, which is critical for next-generation AI accelerators that are increasingly constrained by power delivery and thermal limits.

articles.title: Microsoft and Atom Computing Demonstrate Logical Qubit Error Correction Below Threshold
articles.summary: Microsoft and Atom Computing published results showing their neutral-atom quantum processor achieved logical error rates below the fault-tolerance threshold using a distance-7 surface code with 98 physical qubits per logical qubit. This milestone marks a practical inflection point for quantum error correction, bringing closer the prospect of fault-tolerant quantum computation for chemistry simulation and optimization problems.

articles.title: EU AI Act Enforcement Begins: First Compliance Audits Target Foundation Model Providers
articles.summary: The European Union's AI Office commenced its first formal compliance audits under the AI Act's general-purpose AI provisions, targeting major foundation model providers including OpenAI, Google, and Meta operating in European markets. Companies are required to submit detailed documentation on training data provenance, systemic risk assessments, and energy consumption metrics, with potential fines up to 3% of global annual revenue for non-compliance.
